content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

August 27, 1990

Mr. Jack Harden  
2601 Gordon Avenue  
La Habra, CA 90631

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-498

Dear Mr. Harden:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1/ Your letter dated July 27, 1990, accurately reflects the advice given to you.

Your were advised that although there is no specific provision which covers the method for compensating campaign workers, Section 85802, which governs the use of campaign funds, provides guidance on this matter. Specifically, Section 85802(b) permits the use of campaign funds to pay for the costs of professional services which are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. Work performed to solicit funds for your candidacy by paid employees and county campaign managers is similar to professional services performed in connection with a campaign. Therefore, if the solicitation of funds by the campaign workers is directly related to your candidacy for the Office of Insurance Commissioner, payment based on a percentage of funds solicited by each campaign worker is not prohibited by the Act. It is possible, however, that a question could arise as to whether the compensation is a gift to the employee if the compensation exceeds what is generally paid for comparable work. Thus, in making these payments, you must ensure that they constitute the fair market value for the work performed by each campaign worker.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division
